title: InstCombine: Fold bitcast of vector with constant to scalar
author: arsenm
description:
Fold bitcast (select cond, val, const) ->
  select cond, (bitcast val), (bitcast const)

Rocm device libs has an unfortunate amount of code that does bithacking
on the sign bit of double values by casting to <2 x i32> and operating
on the high element. This breaks value tracking optimizations on the
fp value.

The existing transform would only do this if the input to the select was
also a bitcast with a single use, and if it didn't convert between vector
and scalar.

tests.test_body:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare float @llvm.copysign.f32(float, float) #0

define i32 @issue178245(i32 %i.0.i.i) {
entry:
  %tobool104.i.i.not = icmp eq i32 %i.0.i.i, -1177359834
  %conv111.i.i = select i1 %tobool104.i.i.not, i32 0, i32 -5
  %cast.sign = bitcast i32 %conv111.i.i to float
  %fcmp = fcmp uno float %cast.sign, 0.000000e+00
  %copysign = call float @llvm.copysign.f32(float 0.000000e+00, float %cast.sign)
  %select = select i1 %fcmp, float 0.000000e+00, float %copysign
  %result = bitcast float %select to i32
  ret i32 %result
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

target datalayout = "E-p:64:64:64-p1:64:64:64-a0:0:8-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128"

define <vscale x 2 x i64> @h3(i1 %A, <vscale x 4 x i32> %B) {
EntryBlock:
  %cf = select i1 %A, <vscale x 4 x i32> zeroinitializer, <vscale x 4 x i32> %B
  %bc = bitcast <vscale x 4 x i32> %cf to <vscale x 2 x i64>
  ret <vscale x 2 x i64> %bc
}

comments.author: arsenm
comments.body:
* **#179042** 👈 (View in Graphite) * `main`

This stack of pull requests is managed by Graphite. Learn more about stacking.

comments.author: arsenm
comments.body:
> Your tests convert a vector select into a scalar select, which seems reasonable. But what about the case where this ends up converting a scalar select into a vector select?

It's not producing vector select, it's producing select of vector type. I thought the general cast+select preference was to convert to the destination type (e.g, trunc of select with constant is pushed to select of trunc)

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
> > Your tests convert a vector select into a scalar select, which seems reasonable. But what about the case where this ends up converting a scalar select into a vector select?
> 
> It's not producing vector select, it's producing select of vector type.

Yes, that's what I meant. Replacing a scalar select with a select of vector type may be a significant pessimization, e.g. on x86 the former will use cmov and the latter will use a branch. See https://llvm.godbolt.org/z/WodKcbrbe for an example.

If you want to convert scalar to more expensive vector selects we need to have undo-transforms in the backend in place.

comments.author: arsenm
comments.body: Changed to disallow introducing select of vector, but still allow conversion to select of vector with fewer elements
